title: C# Basic — Paper 9
description: C# concepts: loop control, arrays, variables, strings, nullable types, static members, inheritance basics, enums, structs, and methods.

questions.text: What is the use of a labeled `break` in C#? Does C# support it?

options.text: Yes, C# supports `break label;` to exit a specific outer loop
No, C# does not support labeled break; use goto or restructuring instead
Yes, using `break outer;` syntax
Yes, with `break @label;` syntax

questions.text:
What is the output of:
`for (int i=0; i<3; i++) { for (int j=0; j<3; j++) { if (j==1) continue; Console.Write($"{i}{j} "); } }`
In which scenario is `goto` MOST acceptable in C# code?

options.text: For general code navigation instead of methods
Breaking out of deeply nested loops when alternatives are less clear
For calling methods recursively
In switch expressions as an alternative to `when`

questions.text: What does `Array.Reverse(arr)` do in C#?

options.text: Returns a new reversed array
Reverses the elements of the array in place
Sorts the array in descending order
Throws an exception for arrays with fewer than 2 elements

questions.text: What property gives the number of rows in a 2D array `int[,] arr = new int[3,4]`?

options.text: arr.Length
arr.GetLength(0)
arr.Rows
arr.GetLength(1)

questions.text: What is `Array.IndexOf(arr, value)` used for in C#?

options.text: Returns the element at the given index
Returns the index of the first occurrence of value in arr, or -1 if not found
Returns true if the value exists
Removes the element from arr

questions.text: What is the `float` type's precision compared to `double` in C#?

options.text: float has more precision than double
float (7 significant digits) has less precision than double (15-16 significant digits)
They have the same precision
double has 7 digits; float has 15 digits

questions.text: What suffix is required for a `decimal` literal in C#?

options.text: d or D
f or F
m or M
l or L

questions.text: What is the scope of a variable declared with `var` inside a `for` loop initializer?

options.text: Available throughout the entire method
Available only within the for loop block
Available in the class
Available in all loops in the method

questions.text: What is the result of `"Hello".ToUpper()`?

options.text: hello
The original string is uppercased in place
HELLO
HeLLo

questions.text: What does `string.IsNullOrEmpty(s)` return for `s = ""`?

options.text: false
null
true

questions.text: Which string method checks if a string contains a specific substring in C#?

options.text: str.Has("sub")
str.Includes("sub")
str.Contains("sub")
str.Find("sub")

questions.text: What does `int? x = 5; x = null;` do in C#?

options.text: Compile error: cannot assign null to int?
Sets x to 0
Sets x to null (the nullable int now holds no value)
Runtime NullReferenceException

questions.text: What is the purpose of `Nullable<T>.GetValueOrDefault(defaultValue)` in C#?

options.text: Returns the value if present, otherwise throws an exception
Returns the value if not null; otherwise returns the specified default value
Converts the nullable to a non-nullable permanently
Returns null always

questions.text: What does `string? name = null; Console.WriteLine(name?.Length ?? 0);` output?

options.text: null
Throws NullReferenceException

questions.text: What is the purpose of `Environment.TickCount` as a static property in C#?

options.text: Returns the number of threads running
Returns the number of milliseconds elapsed since the system started; demonstrates static property usage
Returns the current CPU tick
Returns the number of static constructors called

questions.text: Can you call a non-static method from within a static method in the same class in C#?

options.text: Yes, always
No, not directly; you need an instance of the class to call instance methods
Only if the non-static method is public
Only if the class is sealed

questions.text: What is the difference between a static class and a regular class with all static members in C#?

options.text: Static classes enforce that no instances can be created; a regular class with all statics still allows instantiation
They are completely identical
A regular class cannot have all static members
Static classes cannot be used in assemblies

questions.text: In C# inheritance, what is the method resolution order when a method is called on an object?

options.text: The base class version is always called first
The most derived class's override is called first
Both base and derived versions are called
The interface's default implementation is always preferred

questions.text: What is polymorphism in C#?

options.text: The ability to have multiple constructors
The ability to call a method on an object and have the correct overridden version execute based on the actual runtime type
The ability to convert between types
The ability to create many objects of the same class

questions.text: What does `abstract class` mean in C# — can you instantiate it?

options.text: Yes, abstract classes can be instantiated directly
No, abstract classes cannot be instantiated; they must be subclassed
Only with the `new` operator from the same assembly
Only via reflection

questions.text: How do you obtain the string name of an enum value at runtime in C#?

options.text: myEnum.Name
myEnum.ToString()
Enum.GetName(myEnum)
nameof(myEnum)

questions.text: What is the benefit of the `[Flags]` attribute on an enum in C#?

options.text: It prevents combining enum values
It allows enum values to be combined using bitwise operators and formats them nicely with ToString()
It changes the underlying type to long
It generates a Parse method automatically

questions.text: What does `Enum.IsDefined(typeof(Status), 99)` return if 99 is not a valid Status value?

options.text: true
Throws an exception
null
false

questions.text: What limitation do structs have regarding inheritance in C#?

options.text: Structs can inherit from other structs but not classes
Structs cannot inherit from other structs or classes (only implement interfaces)
Structs can inherit from abstract classes
Structs inherit from object by default with virtual method support

questions.text: What method signature demonstrates overloading in C#?

options.text: void Add(int x) and void Add(int x) in the same class
void Add(int x) and int Add(int x) in the same class
void Add(int x) and void Add(double x) in the same class
void Add(int x) in a base class and void Add(int x) in a derived class

questions.text: What is the difference between method overloading and method overriding in C#?

options.text: Overloading: same method name, different signatures in the same class. Overriding: replacing a virtual method in a derived class
Overloading: changing implementation in a derived class. Overriding: different signatures in the same class
They are the same concept
Overloading uses `override` keyword; overriding uses `new` keyword

questions.text: What does `params string[] args` allow when calling the method?

options.text: Only one string argument
Passing zero or more strings as separate arguments or as a string array
Only a pre-allocated string array
No arguments at all

questions.text: What is the resolution rule when both a named argument and a positional argument are provided in a method call in C#?

options.text: Named arguments must always come first
Positional arguments must come before named arguments
Named arguments must always come last, after all positional arguments
They cannot be mixed

questions.text: What is the maximum number of `params` arrays allowed in a single C# method signature?

options.text: Unlimited
1, and it must be the last parameter
1, and it can appear anywhere in the signature
